turnaround_reason: Not a stock — this is a 2x daily inverse gold ETF. It is in a long-term decline because gold has trended up over 4 years (200w MA), making the inverse ETF look like a 'deeply beat-down recovery candidate.' This is the opposite of a turnaround: it's a leveraged inverse instrument doing exactly what it was designed to do.
bullish_points: N/A — leveraged inverse ETF, not an operating business
bearish_points: GLL is structurally designed to decay due to daily reset volatility drag
Owning GLL long-term has been a loss for the entire 4-year window of the screen
Inverse leveraged ETFs are tactical-trading tools, not turnaround equities
Any 'rally' in GLL just means gold pulled back recently — it's a gold-bearish bet, not a quality call
catalyst: Sustained decline in gold prices (a macro-driven view, not a turnaround thesis)
suggested_trade: Skip. Action item — add a filter in src/screener.py to exclude ETFs. yfinance reports a 'quoteType' field that includes 'ETF' vs 'EQUITY'; filter to EQUITY only.
key_risk: N/A — not an operating company; would only be appropriate as a tactical macro hedge
would_skip_if: Always — ETFs should never pass an equity-turnaround screen
